reasons: Lolita reduces Kimmy's poke value by blocking ranged attacks and projectiles from the shield direction.
Kimmy needs stable line access to keep damaging safely, while Lolita forces her to reposition or delay her damage window.

proof.summary: Kimmy wants to pressure with repeated ranged attacks and skill damage, while Lolita can block incoming ranged basic attacks and projectiles from the shield direction.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Kimmy before Kimmy commits her poke window.
Allies stay behind Lolita's shield instead of standing outside its protection.
failureCases: Kimmy's team forces Lolita to use shield for another threat.

reasons: Saber punishes Kimmy when she is isolated or lacks defensive support.
Saber's targeted burst reduces Kimmy's chance to kite through a long fight.

proof.summary: Kimmy wants extended movement-based damage, while Saber can lock her with a targeted airborne burst window before she gets enough time to kite.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits until Kimmy is separated from peel.
Saber engages after Kimmy has used repositioning or defensive tools.
failureCases: Kimmy stays near tank or support protection.
Saber dives into grouped enemies without follow-up.

reasons: Kaja can punish Kimmy's forward positioning when she steps up to maintain damage.
Suppression limits Kimmy's ability to keep moving, attacking, or repositioning during the catch window.

proof.summary: Kimmy depends on movement and spacing, while Kaja can suppress and drag her into allied burst.
worksBestWhen: Kaja's team is close enough to burst Kimmy during suppression.

reasons: Chou can close distance and kick Kimmy out of her safe damage position.
His displacement is useful against a ranged damage dealer who depends on spacing rather than durability.

proof.summary: Kimmy wants to kite while dealing damage, while Chou can use mobility and displacement to isolate her from her team.
worksBestWhen: Chou saves his combo for Kimmy instead of wasting it on the frontline.
failureCases: Chou is forced to engage through multiple peel skills.

reasons: Natalia pressures Kimmy from unseen angles and punishes her before she gets a stable poke angle.
Natalia's stealth threat forces Kimmy to respect bushes and flank routes instead of freely poking.

proof.summary: Kimmy is strongest when she sees the fight and keeps spacing, while Natalia threatens her from concealment and forces Kimmy to respect flank vision.
worksBestWhen: Natalia tracks Kimmy's rotation before teamfights.
Natalia attacks after Kimmy is separated from peel.
failureCases: Kimmy's team controls bushes and flank routes.
Natalia enters after Kimmy's tank has already marked her position.
